sourceMovie.title: Beau Is Afraid
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Beau Is Afraid (2023) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ari Aster's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dogma
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Beau Is Afraid clicked for you, Dogma (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dave Made a Maze
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Beau Is Afraid clicked for you, Dave Made a Maze (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and adventure, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Beau Is Afraid?
faq.answer: Try Dogma, Dave Made a Maze, Dream Scenario—each shares DNA with Beau Is Afraid while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Beau Is Afraid?
faq.answer: Beau Is Afraid (2023) is best described as Comedy, Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Beau Is Afraid worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
